author: intelgaoxiong
title: CachingTest LoadMultiWithConfig_race import_model call count mismatch on Windows
signature: Actual function call count doesnt match EXPECT_CALL import_model Expected called times Actual called twice unsatisfied and active|Flaky Test

failed_jobs.name: C++ unit tests / C++ unit tests
failed_jobs.symptom: CachingTest/CachingTest.LoadMultiWithConfig_race/ByModelName_testCache: import_model expected 3 times but called twice

key_errors:
caching_test.cpp(2667): error: Actual function call count doesn't match EXPECT_CALL(*mockPlugin, import_model(A<std::istream&>(), _))...
         Expected: to be called 3 times
           Actual: called twice - unsatisfied and active

caching_test.cpp(312): error: Value of: Mock::VerifyAndClearExpectations(mockPlugin.get())
  Actual: false
Expected: true
[  FAILED  ] CachingTest/CachingTest.LoadMultiWithConfig_race/ByModelName_testCache, where GetParam() = ((4-byte object <02-00 00-00>, "ByModelName", false), "testCache") (2053 ms)
root_cause: The test CachingTest.LoadMultiWithConfig_race is a race-condition stress test that repeatedly loads models via the MULTI device to exercise caching concurrency. The ByModelName_testCache variant ran 128 iterations over 2 seconds before failing because the import_model mock expectation (set to 3 calls) was only satisfied twice. This is a timing-dependent intermittent failure: the test races between cache-hit reads and cache-write completions, and on a loaded Windows CI runner the timing did not align to produce the expected 3 imports. The PR being merged (#35018, NPUW block-based KV cache) modifies only NPUW LLM pipeline code and is unrelated to the inference-level caching code tested here.
reproduction_steps: Run on Windows with a loaded system: cd build && ctest -R CachingTest/CachingTest.LoadMultiWithConfig_race --repeat until-fail:200
recommended_actions: Re-run the failed job - this is a transient flaky test unrelated to the merged PR
Consider marking CachingTest.LoadMultiWithConfig_race as GTEST_FLAG_SET(death_test_style, threadsafe) or wrapping with retry logic in the test fixture
File a GitHub issue to track this flaky test and add it to the known-flaky list
prevention_strategies: Add CachingTest.LoadMultiWithConfig_race to the known-flaky test list so failures don't block unrelated PRs. Consider using a dedicated flaky-test detection pipeline rather than blocking merge queue on known-intermittent tests.
historical_context: A very similar failure (CachingTest LoadMulti_race import_model call count mismatch) was recorded twice before: run 28366156201 (2026-06-29, PR #36592) and run 28507710758 (2026-07-01, PR #35805), both on Windows C++ unit tests. This confirms it is a pre-existing, recurring flaky test.
